title: Maximize Non-Overlapping Subintervals within Time Constraints

statement.markdown:
Given a list of subintervals [[start1, end1], [start2, end2], ...] with duration end1 - start1, and a time constraint T, 
return the maximum number of non-overlapping subintervals that can be scheduled within the given time constraint T.


Example inputs and outputs are provided in the 'examples' section.
statement.inputFormat: A list of intervals with start and end points, and a time constraint T.
statement.notes: The intervals are non-overlapping, and the order of the subintervals is not important. The function should return the count of non-overlapping subintervals that can be scheduled within the time constraint T.

examples.explanation: Two non-overlapping subintervals [1, 3] and [7, 9] can be scheduled within time constraint 3.
All subintervals can be scheduled within time constraint 5.

hints.text: Consider sorting the subintervals by their end points for a greedy approach.

editorial.title: Maximize Non-Overlapping Subintervals within Time Constraints

functionSignature.python: def schedule_subintervals(self, intervals: list[list[int]], T: int) -> int:
